title: Remove OTP from Registration and Replace with Internet Identity Authentication

requirements.summary: Refactor RegisterPage to a 2-step flow: Step 1 collects Display Name, Mobile Number, and BGMI Player ID with a Continue button; Step 2 shows an Internet Identity authentication prompt instead of OTP verification. Remove all OTP-related UI and logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Registration page shows only 2 steps in the stepper: 'Details' and 'Done'
Step 1 contains Display Name, Mobile Number, and BGMI Player ID fields with a Continue button
Step 2 shows an Internet Identity login button (no OTP input, no OTP banner)
Clicking the Internet Identity button triggers the Internet Identity authentication popup
After successful Internet Identity authentication, the player profile is registered on-chain using the authenticated identity
No 'Demo OTP: 1234' banner or OTP input field exists anywhere in the registration flow
The 'Unauthorized: Only authenticated users can register as a player' error no longer appears because Internet Identity is authenticated before the registerPlayer call is made

file_operations.description: Refactor the RegisterPage component to implement a 2-step registration flow. Step 1: Collect Display Name, Mobile Number, and BGMI Player ID with a 'Continue' button that advances to Step 2. Step 2: Display an Internet Identity authentication button using the existing useInternetIdentity hook. Remove the entire OTP verification step (Step 2 'Verify OTP'), including the OTP input field, the 'Demo OTP: 1234' banner, and all calls to generateOtp and verifyOtp mutations. Update the stepper UI to show only 2 steps labeled 'Details' and 'Done'. After successful Internet Identity authentication in Step 2, call the registerPlayer backend capability with the collected user details and the authenticated identity. Handle the registration success by logging the user into the player session (via AuthContext) and navigating to the player dashboard. Remove all OTP-related state variables, mutation hooks (useGenerateOtp, useVerifyOtp), and conditional rendering logic related to OTP. Ensure error handling displays appropriate messages if Internet Identity authentication fails or if registerPlayer returns an error. Use the authorization component to ensure the user is authenticated before calling registerPlayer, preventing the 'Unauthorized: Only authenticated users can register as a player' error.

Remove the useGenerateOtp and useVerifyOtp mutation hooks from this file, as they are no longer needed after removing OTP-based registration. Ensure all other query and mutation hooks remain intact and functional. This cleanup prevents unused backend capabilities from being exposed in the frontend codebase.
